title: Volumes, PersistentVolumes, Claims, StorageClasses, CSI, Snapshots, and Recovery · Reconciliation Brief: K8s App Consistent Backup Restore
summary: A stateful intake service binds storage in the wrong zone, deletes data through a reclaim policy, and treats a crash-consistent snapshot as a proven application restore. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s app consistent backup restore increment in stateful storage and recovery contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.
Explain CSI controller and node roles, drivers, snapshots, clones, expansion, topology, credentials, feature support, and deprecated in-tree migration boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s App Consistent Backup Restore · Theory K8s App Consistent Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation. A defensible solution applies K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to stateful storage and recovery contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-storage-persistence-recovery-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-storage-persistence-recovery-4
    artifact: storage-recovery.yaml
    decision: worked k8s storage persistence recovery 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      claim: intake-data
      storageClass: retained-zonal
      volumeBindingMode: WaitForFirstConsumer
      reclaimPolicy: Retain
      snapshot: application-quiesced
      restore: fresh-namespace-and-claim
      checksum: verified
    verify: kubectl get pv,pvc,storageclass,volumesnapshot -A -o json
    failure: Reject Bound as durability proof, Delete reclaim surprises, arbitrary-zone binding, snapshot-as-backup claims, or restore without application validation.
    repair: Trace claim and volume lifecycle, align topology and access, quiesce the application, protect metadata and keys, and rehearse clean restore.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A Bound PVC and successful VolumeSnapshot prove the application can recover within its objectives. This fails the K8s App Consistent Backup Restore contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
